CAN: construct Riemannian manifolds (S^n, SO(n), SPD matrices, hyperbolic)
compute exponential and logarithm maps on manifolds
compute geodesic distances between points on manifolds
support numpy and PyTorch backends interchangeably
handle parallel transport and Fréchet mean on curved spaces
CANNOT: compute geometric algebra products (use clifford for that)
prove geometric properties formally (use z3 for that)
handle discrete topology (use toponetx/gudhi for that)
guarantee geodesic uniqueness near cut loci (conjugate points)

pytorch.reason: geomstats supports numpy backend; pytorch backend optional and not needed for this capability probe.

pyg.reason: not used: this probe isolates geomstats differential geometry capabilities alone; cross-tool coupling is deferred to a separate integration sim per the four-sim-kinds doctrine (capability vs integration separation).

s2_construction.detail: Hypersphere(dim=2) is the 2-sphere embedded in R^3

exp_map_stays_on_manifold.detail: exp_map from north pole + tangent must land on S^2

log_inv_exp.detail: log(exp(v, p), p) = v (log is inverse of exp on S^2)

geodesic_distance.detail: Geodesic distance between [1,0,0] and [0,1,0] on S^2 = pi/2

antipodal_max_distance.detail: Antipodal distance on S^2 = pi (max geodesic distance)

zero_tangent_exp.detail: exp(0, p) = p: zero tangent maps to same point

s1_circle.detail: Hypersphere(dim=1) is S^1 (circle); [1,0] belongs to it

zero_distance_self.detail: dist(p, p) = 0 on any Riemannian manifold
